content.problemStatement:
There are two sequences A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​. Answer QQQ queries on these sequences.

In each query, you should calculate the dot product of subsequences Ax,Ax+1,…,Ax+l−1A_x, A_{x+1}, \dots, A_{x+l-1}Ax​,Ax+1​,…,Ax+l−1​ and By,By+1,…,By+l−1B_y, B_{y+1}, \dots, B_{y+l-1}By​,By+1​,…,By+l−1​, i.e. the value of the expression
∑k=0l−1Ax+kBy+k .\sum_{k=0}^{l-1} A_{x+k} B_{y+k} \,.∑k=0l−1​Ax+k​By+k​.
constraints: 1 ≤ Ai, Bi ≤ 10^4 for each valid i
The sum of N for all test cases does not exceed 10^5
The sum of Q for all test cases does not exceed 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and Q. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN. The third line contains N space-separated integers B1, B2, …, BN. Each of the following Q lines contains three space-separated integers x, y, and l describing one query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer — the answer to the query (the dot product).

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first query, the subsequences are [6, 5, 2] and [2, 4, 6]. The dot product is (6 * 2) + (5 * 4) + (2 * 6) = 12 + 20 + 12 = 44. For the second query, the subsequences are [4] and [3]. The dot product is (4 * 3) = 12.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can iterate over each query and calculate the dot product of the specified subsequences. We can do this by iterating over the range of the subsequence and summing the products of the corresponding elements from the two sequences.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * Q * l) — where T is the number of test cases, Q is the number of queries, and l is the length of the subsequence. This is because for each query, we are iterating over the subsequence to calculate the dot product.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — excluding the space required for the input and output, the space complexity is constant because we are only using a constant amount of space to store the variables.

code.Python3:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
for _ in range(int(input())):
    N, Q = map(int, input().split())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    B = list(map(int, input().split()))
    for _ in range(Q):
        x, y, l = map(int, input().split())
        sum = 0
        for i in range(l):
            sum += A[x + i - 1] * B[y + i - 1]
        print(sum)
